shape_semantics: Joint-necessity. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,L,W,_ : ONLY value_profile_cmplog resolves; cmplog blocks AND value_profile blocks; naive is NON-decisive. The vpc-only resolution asserts BOTH I2S and the CMP_MAP gradient are jointly required. Every branch carries TWO Pass-A signatures of ONE joint mechanism (an h0 I2S half + an h1 gradient half), except harfbuzz/7170 which the classifier kept as a single joint signature. Layer 1 already asserts jointness; the evidence test does NOT re-confirm it (that would violate G2). The test discriminates WHICH joint SUBTYPE each branch is, on a measurement INDEPENDENT of the resolve pattern: it reads the structural/value/byte content of the cmplog-BLOCKING and value_profile-BLOCKING seed sets and asks what the gradient adds OVER I2S-only and what I2S adds OVER gradient-only.

hypotheses.label: vpc assembles a LARGER, multi-field structure than I2S-only (cmplog) ever reaches; the gradient is necessary to grow size/token-count past where exact substitution stalls, and I2S is necessary because the gradient alone (value_profile) places ~0 structural tokens
hypotheses.prediction: On the value_profile-BLOCKING seed set the structural-token count is ~0 (vp_tags ~ 0): the gradient alone cannot place ANY exact multi-byte structural token (FOURCC table tag, ICC colorspace signature, XML markup literal), so I2S is necessary. AND the vpc-RESOLVING median seed is materially BIGGER than the cmplog-BLOCKING median seed (size_lift >= 1.3): the gradient grows the multi-field header/content-model structure past where exact-substitution-only (cmplog) plateaus, so the gradient is necessary. A pure-I2S-substitution mechanism (no assembly depth) predicts size_lift ~ 1.0; a pure-gradient mechanism predicts vp_tags > 0. Only the assembly-depth joint predicts BOTH vp_tags~0 AND size_lift>=1.3.

hypotheses.label: vpc and cmplog reach a SIMILARLY-sized input (size_lift ~ 1), but vpc lands a specific gradient-climbed VALUE (a single exact multi-byte keyword/token) that the cmplog-blocking seeds approach byte-by-byte yet never reach; I2S is still necessary to splice the keyword operand, and the gradient is necessary for the last-mile prefix precision that the structural size proxy is blind to

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool. For the branch's decisive CMP site, take the target operand value from the signature (the exact SQL keyword/identifier token, e.g. 'PRAGMA' = 50 52 41 47 4d 41 for all four sqlite3 branches; operand_width_bytes=6). Compute the MIN byte-distance (Hamming over the operand-width window) between the target value and the value found at the cmp-site offset across (a) the vpc-RESOLVING seed set => vpc_min_distance and (b) the cmplog-BLOCKING seed set => cmp_min_distance. distance_closure_ratio = (cmp_min_distance - vpc_min_distance) / max(cmp_min_distance, 1).
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes ('PRAGMA', 6); cmp-site offset is the input head (per the cards), confirmed via per-role coverage overlay / analysis byte-diff
params.feasibility: All four value-precision members are sqlite3, which HAS an on-disk corpus AND is among the 286 reps with resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in the DB, so they are scorable.

hypotheses.prediction: Inputs are the SAME size (size_lift ~ 1.0 -- a 'PRAGMA <name>;' statement is no bigger than the SELECT/junk blocking fragments) BUT the vpc-resolving seeds reach a keyword value strictly CLOSER to the exact target token than the cmplog-blocking seeds ever do: cmp_min_distance > 0 (cmplog seeds prefix-collide on a byte or two of 'PRAGMA' but never accrete the full keyword) while vpc_min_distance ~ 0, so distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S mechanism would predict cmp_min_distance ~ 0 (cmplog HAS I2S; if one substitution sufficed cmplog would resolve, contradicting L). This rules out 'I2S alone is enough' AND, because size_lift~1, rules out assembly-depth.

hypotheses.label: ROUND-1 hypothesis (KEEP). The decisive divergence is on a DERIVED-INTEGER header/count field reached over a range/inequality gate: the literal table tag is PRESENT in BOTH arms (so assembly-depth's vp_tags<0.3 fails) and input size does NOT lift (so size_lift>=1.3 fails), yet vpc resolves because the CMP_MAP gradient hill-climbs the multi-byte NUMERIC offset/count/length fields toward a jointly-consistent in-range configuration that single-shot I2S substitution (cmplog) cannot reach. I2S is still necessary to plant the dispatching tag; the gradient is necessary for the numeric magnitude convergence that neither the size proxy NOR a fixed-literal value-distance can see (the target is a derived integer with no input literal). NOTE (round 2): on real data this rule did NOT fire for harfbuzz/6858 or harfbuzz/7245 (round-1 arbiter: scored, none held) -- the derived-integer numeric distance gap could not be anchored without an input literal, so those two branches were re-routed to joint_fixed_offset_literal_presence below. The hypothesis is carried forward VERBATIM (superset/freeze) so any branch it confirmed re-validates; no threshold was relaxed.

params.comment: Reuses value_distance_reached in its NUMERIC mode (the tool already supports 'magnitude gap for inequality gates'). The gate is an inequality/range on a DERIVED INTEGER. The target is NOT a fixed input literal (operand_literal=null, operand_kind=derived_integer), so this is a genuinely DIFFERENT measurement from joint_value_precision (which needs a fixed multi-byte literal operand): here the tool computes, per arm, the MIN magnitude gap of the wide LE integer header fields versus the in-range value the parser requires, over the cmplog-BLOCKING vs vpc-RESOLVING seed sets.
params.site_source: decisive cmp site from the h1 (gradient) signature/card; offsets confirmed via per-role coverage overlay / analysis byte-diff.
params.feasibility: harfbuzz HAS an on-disk corpus AND resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds in the DB. On real data this rule did not anchor for 6858/7245 (the in-range value is computed, not present in the input); see round-2 re-routing to literal-presence.

hypotheses.prediction: vp_tags > 0.3 (the table tag IS present in the value_profile-blocking seeds too, so assembly-depth's vp_tags<0.3 fails) AND size_lift < 1.3 (no structural growth), so BOTH prior joint rules miss. INDEPENDENTLY, on the derived-integer header field the vpc-resolving arm lands a magnitude STRICTLY closer to the in-range configuration than the cmplog-blocking arm: cmp_min_distance > 0 while vpc_min_distance is much smaller, so winner_closer=true and distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S story predicts cmp_min_distance ~ 0. An assembly-depth story predicts size_lift>=1.3. A fixed-literal value-precision story is inapplicable. Only the numeric-header-convergence joint predicts size_lift<1.3 AND vp_tags>=0.3 AND a numeric distance gap on the derived-integer field.
hypotheses.rule: size_lift < 1.3 AND vp_tags >= 0.3 AND cmp_min_distance > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.5 AND winner_closer == true

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 2, KEEP). The decisive joint divergence is GATED BY A FIXED-OFFSET CONTIGUOUS MULTI-BYTE LITERAL (a FOURCC table tag at a constant low input offset) where the BLOCKING corpus carries JUNK/absence at that offset: the value_profile-BLOCKING corpus is DEPLETED of the exact tag bytes at that offset (it carries non-tag decoy bytes -- 'fe ff ff ff', 'GOS' fragments, zeros), while the vpc-RESOLVING (I2S-bearing) corpus is ENRICHED for the exact literal there. The binding constraint the gradient-only arm cannot clear is the EXACT-BYTE PLACEMENT at a fixed offset, not a numeric magnitude (numeric_header_convergence failed for these on real data) and not seed growth (assembly_depth's size_lift). I2S is necessary because only the I2S-bearing arms place the literal; the gradient is necessary downstream but the OFFSET-LITERAL presence is the measurable, discriminating axis here.

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT operand_enrichment / byte_freq_ratio tool. Per W/L gate offset it measures the fraction of the RESOLVING (W) vs BLOCKING (L) corpus whose bytes equal the target literal vs a decoy. The fixed-offset contiguous literal makes byte alignment valid even though these are joint gates -- the data_realities byte-alignment caveat is about DIFFERENT-SIZED structural-assembly gates where the literal floats; here the tag sits at a CONSTANT low offset regardless of seed size, so the gate-offset measurement is well-defined. NOTE (round 3): lcms/2674 was REMOVED from this hypothesis. Its blocking corpus does NOT carry junk/absence at the tag offset -- it carries a DIFFERENT VALID 4-byte ICC FOURCC ('mntr'/'RGB '/'XYZ ') at the same offset, so byte_freq_ratio's target-vs-decoy enrichment came out below threshold (the offset is occupied by valid signature bytes in both arms; only WHICH signature differs). That is a discrete-literal SELECTION regime, re-routed to joint_discrete_literal_selection below.
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes (h0 I2S half); fixed offset confirmed via the card byte-diff (W-vs-L concentrated byte set at the named offset: 6858 'typ1' at 0x00 + 'loca' at 0x0c, L carries 'fe ff ff ff'/'e0 17 00 00' junk there; 7245 same, L carries zeros/0xe0/'GPOS' fragments).
params.feasibility: harfbuzz/6858 + harfbuzz/7245 HAVE an on-disk corpus on s4 and are scorable here. operand_enrichment needs >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in the DB (the reps have them).

hypotheses.prediction: At the fixed tag offset the RESOLVING corpus carries the exact literal far more than the BLOCKING corpus does: signed_target_enrich > 0 (the value_profile-blocking corpus is depleted of the exact FOURCC -- it holds JUNK/absence decoy bytes -- while the I2S-bearing resolving corpus concentrates the literal), and the decoy bytes dominate the blocking corpus (decoy_enrich at the offset). A pure-NUMERIC-convergence mechanism (the refuted joint_numeric_header_convergence story for these branches) predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0: the literal would be present in BOTH arms and the divergence would be only in the wide integer fields. An assembly-depth mechanism predicts the divergence shows up as seed-size growth, not offset-literal enrichment. A discrete-selection mechanism (the lcms case) predicts signed_target_enrich ~ 0 because the offset is occupied by a valid-but-WRONG literal in the blocking arm. Only the junk/absence fixed-offset-literal-presence mechanism predicts a positive signed_target_enrich AT the constant tag offset. This is FALSIFIABLE (it could come out ~0) AND DISCRIMINATING (rules out numeric-convergence, assembly-depth, AND discrete-selection) AND G2-INDEPENDENT (it reads byte content at a fixed offset in the BLOCKING vs RESOLVING corpus, never the resolve label).

hypotheses.label: NEW (round 3). The decisive joint divergence is a DISCRETE-LITERAL SELECTION at a fixed offset: the gate compares a fixed-offset multi-byte field against ONE specific signature out of a small set of VALID alternatives. Unlike fixed_offset_literal_presence (where the blocking corpus carries junk/absence at the offset), here BOTH arms place a well-formed multi-byte literal at the same offset -- the value_profile-BLOCKING corpus carries a DIFFERENT but valid ICC FOURCC ('mntr'/'RGB '/'XYZ '), while only the vpc-RESOLVING (I2S-bearing) corpus lands the SPECIFIC target signature ('prtr'/'Lab '). The binding constraint the gradient-only arm cannot clear is not byte-presence (the offset is occupied in both arms) but EXACT SELECTION of the routing signature among several valid options. The measurement axis is therefore the Hamming DISTANCE between the value at the offset and the exact target literal, NOT a target-vs-decoy byte-presence enrichment (which is blind here because both arms occupy the offset with valid signature bytes).

params.comment: Reuses the BUILT value_distance_reached tool, keyed on the exact fixed-offset ICC FOURCC literal ('prtr' = 70 72 74 72 at offset 0x0c, width 4; secondary 'Lab ' = 4c 61 62 20 at 0x10). For each arm, take the MIN over seeds of the Hamming distance (over the 4-byte operand window at the fixed offset) between the value at the offset and the target literal. The vpc-RESOLVING arm lands the exact signature => vpc_min_distance ~ 0; the value_profile-BLOCKING arm carries a DIFFERENT valid ICC FOURCC ('mntr' = 6d 6e 74 72) => a NON-ZERO Hamming distance ('mntr' vs 'prtr' differs in 2 of 4 bytes => ~0.5). distance_closure_ratio = (block_min_distance - vpc_min_distance) / max(block_min_distance, 1).
params.operand_source: signature.operand_literal / operand_width_bytes (h0 I2S half: 'prtr', width 4); fixed offset 0x0c confirmed via the card byte-diff (W='prtr' at 0x0c, L='mntr'/'RGB '/'XYZ ' at the same offset).
params.why_different_from_value_precision: joint_value_precision targets a SLIDING-window keyword whose blocking arm has only a 1-2 byte PREFIX collision and never accretes the full token (cmp_min_distance close to the full width). joint_discrete_literal_selection targets a FIXED-OFFSET field already occupied by a valid alternative literal whose distance to the target is a SMALL fixed value (~0.5 for one differing pair) -- a discrete swap, not a byte-by-byte prefix climb. Same tool, different operand regime: this is why value_precision's keyword-at-input-head framing did not anchor lcms/2674 in round 1, and why operand_enrichment's byte-presence framing did not anchor it in round 2.
params.feasibility: lcms/2674's corpus is sB-owned (lcms,libxml2,libpng,bloaty on sB). The test is designed valid for the branch (cross-server rule): sB HAS lcms's resolving_seeds+blocking_seeds among the 286 reps in db/blockers.sqlite, so value_distance_reached scores it from the DB seed sets; sB arbitrates lcms. NOT marked decidable:false for off-server reasons.

hypotheses.prediction: At the fixed signature offset the value_profile-BLOCKING arm holds a VALID but WRONG ICC FOURCC, so its value is a SHORT Hamming distance from the target literal but strictly > 0 (block_min_distance ~ 0.5: the blocking seeds keep 'mntr'/'RGB '/'XYZ ' where the target is 'prtr'), while the vpc-RESOLVING (I2S-bearing) arm lands the exact 'prtr'/'Lab ' signature => vpc_min_distance ~ 0 and winner_closer=true, so distance_closure_ratio is large. A pure-I2S 'one substitution suffices' story predicts the BLOCKING arm (cmplog has I2S too -- wait, here the L arm is value_profile, which lacks I2S) would also reach distance 0; but value_profile, lacking I2SRandReplace, cannot splice the discrete signature and stalls at a valid-wrong FOURCC. A byte-PRESENCE-enrichment story (fixed_offset_literal_presence) predicts signed_target_enrich > 0, but it came out ~0 here because the offset is OCCUPIED by valid signature bytes in both arms -- which is exactly why this discrete-SELECTION distance test is the discriminating measurement. This is FALSIFIABLE (block_min_distance could come out 0 if the blocking corpus already carried 'prtr', or vpc_min_distance could be > 0 if vpc never landed the exact signature) AND DISCRIMINATING (rules out byte-presence-enrichment, numeric-convergence, and assembly-depth) AND G2-INDEPENDENT (reads the byte value at a fixed offset in the BLOCKING vs RESOLVING seed sets, never the resolve label).
hypotheses.rule: vpc_min_distance < 0.25 AND cmp_min_distance > 0 AND distance_closure_ratio >= 0.5 AND winner_closer == true

hypotheses.label: NON-DISCRIMINABLE (honest inconclusive; FINAL). The libxml2 automaton/grammar-state branches (xmlFAGenerateEpsilonTransition to==NULL; xmlAutomataNewEpsilon to==NULL; xmlParseElementStart nsNr != ctxt->nsNr) resolve because vpc reaches a NON-NULL automaton target state via a GRAMMAR-PATH choice (the optional-quantifier OPT content model vs the MULT model; a namespaced empty-element close), not via a bigger seed, a closer fixed literal, an in-range derived integer, a fixed-offset junk-vs-tag swap, or a discrete fixed-offset signature selection. The decisive feature is WHICH parse production the corpus reached -- a non-local grammar-state property no built tool measures independently of the resolve label.

params.why_undecidable: No BUILT measurement discriminates the joint SUBTYPE here without re-measuring the resolve pattern (G2 violation). (1) The libxml2 gates (7296 xmlFAGenerateEpsilonTransition to==NULL; 7311 xmlAutomataNewEpsilon to==NULL; 9361 xmlParseElementStart nsNr!=ctxt->nsNr) hinge on reaching a NON-NULL automaton state via a grammar production choice (OPT vs MULT content model; a namespaced empty-element close). XML markup literals are present in BOTH W and L seeds and float at VARIABLE positions (byte_signature=dispersed_multibyte, operand_kind=presence_or_nonnull/derived_integer), so: assembly_depth misses (vp_tags not ~0, size barely lifts); value_precision and numeric_header miss (no fixed multi-byte literal / computed in-range target); fixed_offset_literal_presence (byte_freq_ratio) is INAPPLICABLE because the markup literal has NO fixed gate offset (the tool returns no_gate_signature for floating/structural literals); and discrete_literal_selection is INAPPLICABLE for the same reason (value_distance_reached needs a target operand at a determinable cmp-site offset, but operand_literal is null for the gradient halves and 9361's 'xmlns' floats). depth_reach at the branch line would just re-detect that vpc reaches the false side, i.e. verify vpc-resolves with vpc-reaches -- a G2 violation, not an independent signal. There is no built tool that reads 'which grammar production the corpus exercised' as a signal independent of the resolve label. CROSS-SERVER NOTE: these are NOT marked undecidable because libxml2 is off the running server (it is sB-owned with DB seeds) -- they are undecidable because the MECHANISM is non-local/grammar-state with no independent built measurement. The owning server may revisit if a grammar-path / production-coverage descriptor is later built. This is the FINAL round; an honest inconclusive (G3) is the correct, terminal outcome for these three.

hypotheses.prediction: No falsifiable real-data prediction over named metrics is available that is independent of the resolve pattern; these branches are honestly left inconclusive (G3).

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' descriptor is used (I2S is untagged across all 4 fuzzers).

value_profile treated as a FEEDBACK, not a mutator -- its effect is measured only via corpus admission / what the corpus reaches (size, tokens, value-distance, numeric magnitude, byte-presence-at-offset), never a lineage gradient event.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is used ONLY where the literal sits at a CONSTANT low offset AND the blocking corpus carries junk/absence there (the harfbuzz typ1/loca FOURCC at 0x00/0x0c with junk-bearing blocking seeds) so target-vs-decoy enrichment is well-defined -- the data_realities caveat against byte_freq_ratio on joint gates is about DIFFERENT-SIZED structural-assembly gates where the literal FLOATS. The lcms discrete-selection case (where BOTH arms occupy the offset with a valid FOURCC) is NOT scored by byte_freq_ratio -- it uses value_distance_reached (Hamming distance to the exact target signature), the correct tool when the offset is occupied by a valid-but-wrong literal. The floating-literal libxml2 grammar gates are left decidable:false (no fixed gate offset => no_gate_signature; no determinable cmp-site operand => value_distance inapplicable).

struct_size/token_count (assembly), Hamming-window value_distance at the input head (value-precision keyword climb), numeric magnitude gap (header-convergence), fixed-offset junk-vs-tag byte_freq_ratio (literal-presence), and fixed-offset Hamming-distance value_distance (discrete-literal selection) are the five measurement families used -- each chosen to match the operand kind AND the blocking-arm content of its sub-group.

on-disk corpora exist only for curl/harfbuzz/openthread/sqlite3: harfbuzz (assembly + numeric-header + literal-presence) and sqlite3 (value-precision) are scorable on s4; libxml2 + lcms are sB-owned and seed_set/corpus-sourced from the DB reps -- lcms/2674 is decidable on MECHANISM grounds (discrete fixed-offset signature selection, value_distance_reached over DB seed sets) and sB arbitrates it; libxml2 members are decidable:false on MECHANISM grounds (grammar-state), NOT corpus-location grounds.
seed-dependent tools read resolving_seeds + blocking_seeds (or saved corpus) from db/blockers.sqlite (the 286 reps have them).
notes:
ROUND 3 (FINAL) re-design (loop-until-dry). TARGET = the 4 still-unlabeled rule_not_met branches (lcms/2674, libxml2/7296, libxml2/7311, libxml2/9361).

PRIORS KEPT (superset/freeze, VERBATIM, no threshold relaxed): all five round-2 KEEP hypotheses carried forward unchanged so every previously-confirmed branch re-validates -- joint_assembly_depth ('vp_tags<0.3 AND size_lift>=1.3'), joint_value_precision ('size_lift<1.3 AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5'), joint_numeric_header_convergence ('size_lift<1.3 AND vp_tags>=0.3 AND cmp_min_distance>0 AND distance_closure_ratio>=0.5 AND winner_closer==true'), and joint_fixed_offset_literal_presence ('signed_target_enrich>=1.0'). The ONLY change to a prior is administrative and label-preserving: lcms/2674 was REMOVED from joint_fixed_offset_literal_presence's member_hint_branches (it never held under that rule on real data -- its offset is occupied by valid signature bytes in both arms, so byte-presence enrichment is structurally ~0); the RULE 'signed_target_enrich>=1.0' is UNTOUCHED, so harfbuzz/6858 and harfbuzz/7245 (the branches it actually confirms) re-validate identically. member_hint_branches is a routing hint, not a threshold, so this respects the superset/freeze invariant.

NEW MECHANISM (1, discriminable): joint_discrete_literal_selection for lcms/2674. PRINCIPLED, CARD-READABLE reason it failed every round-1/round-2 rule: its decisive operand is a FIXED-OFFSET 4-byte ICC FOURCC ('prtr' at 0x0c, 'Lab ' at 0x10) where the value_profile-BLOCKING corpus carries a DIFFERENT but VALID signature ('mntr'/'RGB '/'XYZ ') at the SAME offset -- not junk, not absence. So (a) assembly_depth misses (no size growth, valid FOURCC in both arms => vp_tags not ~0); (b) value_precision misses (the target is at a FIXED offset, not a sliding keyword at the input head; the blocking arm has a valid-wrong literal, not a 1-2-byte prefix collision); (c) numeric_header misses (the operand is a literal, not a derived integer); (d) fixed_offset_literal_presence's byte_freq_ratio comes out ~0 because the offset is OCCUPIED by valid signature bytes in BOTH arms (target-vs-decoy enrichment only fires when the blocking arm has junk/absence there, as in harfbuzz). The genuinely DIFFERENT, discriminating measurement is value_distance_reached keyed on the EXACT target FOURCC at the FIXED offset: vpc lands distance 0, the value_profile-blocking arm stalls at a valid-wrong FOURCC ('mntr' vs 'prtr' = 2/4 bytes differ => ~0.5). G1: FALSIFIABLE (vpc_min_distance could be >0, or block_min_distance could be 0 if the blocking corpus already held 'prtr') AND DISCRIMINATING (a byte-presence story predicts signed_target_enrich>0 which it does NOT show; a numeric story predicts no literal distance gap; an assembly story predicts size growth). G2: reads the byte value at a fixed offset in the BLOCKING vs RESOLVING seed sets, never the resolve label. NOVELTY: this is NOT a relaxed re-test of any refuted rule -- value_precision used a SLIDING-window keyword at the input head with a prefix-climb closure; this uses a FIXED-OFFSET discrete-signature Hamming distance with a hard vpc_min_distance<0.25 AND winner_closer gate (a stricter, different rule keyed on a different operand regime). The rule's leading metric vpc_min_distance is disjoint from value_precision's leading cmp_min_distance gate, and from literal_presence's signed_target_enrich, so it cannot accidentally re-fire a prior branch.

HONEST decidable:false SUB-GROUP (G3, FINAL): joint_grammar_state_path covers libxml2/7296, libxml2/7311, libxml2/9361 -- unchanged from round 2 (lcms/2674 already moved out in round 2; it stays out and is now in discrete_literal_selection). These three are automaton non-NULL-state / namespace-count gates resolved via a GRAMMAR-PATH choice (OPT vs MULT content model; namespaced empty-element close) -- a non-local grammar-state property. Their markup literals FLOAT (dispersed_multibyte, no fixed gate offset), so byte_freq_ratio is inapplicable (no_gate_signature), value_distance_reached has no determinable fixed cmp-site operand (operand_literal null on the gradient halves; 9361's 'xmlns' floats), value_precision/numeric_header need a fixed literal / computed integer they don't have, and depth_reach at the branch line would re-detect vpc-reaches-false-side (G2 violation). No built tool reads 'which grammar production the corpus exercised' independently of the resolve label, so an honest inconclusive is the correct, TERMINAL outcome. NOT decidable:false for off-server reasons (sB owns libxml2 with DB seeds) -- decidable:false on MECHANISM grounds. This is the final round; these three are dry.

DECISION ORDER: assembly_depth, value_precision, numeric_header_convergence, fixed_offset_literal_presence (all unchanged), then joint_discrete_literal_selection appended last. The new rule's leading metric (vpc_min_distance with winner_closer) is disjoint from every prior rule's gating metric, and the priors that could score lcms/2674 already miss it on real data (value_precision needs a sliding-head keyword closure; literal_presence needs junk-bearing blocking corpus), so order is robust and no prior label is perturbed. Anything matching none falls through to inconclusive; the grammar-state group never scores (rule:false) and lands in inconclusive by design.

LOOP STATUS: this shape is now DRY. Of the 4 round-3 targets, 1 (lcms/2674) gets a new discriminable mechanism (joint_discrete_literal_selection); 3 (libxml2/7296, 7311, 9361) are terminally decidable:false (grammar-state, non-local, no independent built measurement).
